$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace; queryId + artisanpack/postPreview added to usesContext. attribute schema and supports carried verbatim.

Previews via createEntityPlaceholderEdit reading the block's own `content` attribute (with a `Read more` dummy when empty). Upstream edit.js renders a `RichText` editor in-place; the fork's preview is a styled chip — the configured `content` round-trips through block storage as a normal attribute, so authors can still edit it via the InspectorControls panel.
Dynamic block — returns null; markup produced server-side from the block's `content` attribute + stamped `_resolvedPermalink`.
Bidirectional core/read-more ↔ artisanpack/read-more block transforms for the rollout window.
Inline SVG inserter icon (editor lacks dashicons.css).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx replaces upstream's in-place RichText editing surface with a stamped-attribute preview chip — the `content` attribute is still editable via the Inspector and round-trips through block storage unchanged.
read-more is server-rendered: the front-end markup is produced by the Blade/React/Vue renderers from the block's `content` attribute + the stamped `_resolvedPermalink`, not by save().
Upstream ships no `deprecated.js`; the fork mirrors that — read-more landed in a single revision and has no legacy migration.
